title: pseudocalyx macrophyllus
intro: Pseudocalyx macrophyllus, also known as the bush apple, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pseudocalyx macrophyllus include its ability to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections. Pseudocalyx macrophyllus can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves, bark, and roots can be used in herbal preparations to leverage its medicinal properties.
